Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs As Prevailing Party On Petition For Release Of Property From Mechanics Lien (Notice)
Off Calendar Jun-09-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jamir Couche, 415.956.6413 of Anderson, Poole & Couche., P.C. appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff Solomon Varon.
No other appearances.

Discussed. Mr. Couche asks the Court a clarifying question.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS AS PREVAILING PARTY ON PETITION FOR RELEASE OF PROPERTY FROM MECHANICS LIEN is GRANTED, in part. No opposition filed. Petitioner is awarded $7,000 in attorneys' fees as the prevailing party under CCP Section 8488(c). The Court finds the amount of time counsel declares to have spent on certain tasks unreasonable and/or could have been done by a paralegal. These include: (1) the amount of attorney time declared to "coordinate service" over the course of 13 days between 12/15/21 and 12/28/21; (2) the amount of time declared to "finalize and file" a Notice of Hearing and proposed order on 1/28/22; (3) the amount of time declared to draft the ex parte application on 2/9/22; (4) the amount of time declared to prepare for the ex parte hearing. Petitioners are awarded $507 in costs pursuant to CCP Sections 1032 and 1033.5. Petitioners have not established the additional costs associated with the filing fees (e.g., the $225 fee to file the petition versus Petitioner's requested amount of $279). Counsel must provide evidence of the fees charged by their electronic filing service provider for the electronic filing or service of their pleadings. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
